message.content: We need a cache layer for the API responses. What should we use?

content.text: I'll use in-memory LRU caching because it is simple, has no infra overhead, and our service is single-process. Rejecting Redis because it adds an operational dependency we don't currently have.

message.content: Hold on — we run three replicas behind a load balancer, cache coherence matters. And we already operate Redis for the session store.

content.text: You're right, I had the deployment topology wrong. Switching to Redis for the cache layer: it shares state across replicas so cache hits benefit all three, and since Redis is already in the operational footprint there is no new infra cost. In-memory LRU would cause inconsistent responses depending on which replica served the request.
60-second TTL on the Redis entries — balances freshness against the load reduction we're trying to achieve.
Implementing in src/cache/redis-cache.ts with a 60s TTL.
